title: Malaxis discolor
intro: Malaxis discolor, also known as the yellow-fringed orchid, is a type of terrestrial orchid native to North America. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Malaxis discolor include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Malaxis discolor is often made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use and availability may vary depending on regional regulations.
taxonomy: Malaxis discolor is classified as a species of orchid. It belongs to the genus Malaxis, which is a group of terrestrial orchids. The genus Malaxis is part of the subtribe Malaxidinae, within the orchid family Orchidaceae. This classification places Malaxis discolor within the larger family of flowering plants, angiosperms. The species is often referred to by its synonym, Microstylis discolor.
